verdict_reasoning: BAX is a cytosolic/mitochondrial-outer-membrane pro-apoptotic BCL2 family effector. During apoptosis, BAX oligomerizes and permeabilizes the MOM (MOMP). There is documented externalization/exposure of BAX-related epitopes on the outer leaflet of apoptotic cells, and apoptosis-induced surface display of normally intracellular BCL2-family/mitochondrial proteins is a recognized phenomenon (ICD context). Baseline localization is intracellular (cytosol/MOM), so no canonical surface pool exists; surface accessibility is restricted to programmed cell death states. Not pMHC-only since the protein body itself can be exposed on dying cells.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether BAX protein body is robustly displayed on the outer PM leaflet of apoptotic cells vs. only conformationally exposed at the MOM.
